title: Pulling Pan-Seared Mexican Cheese Paella
description: A yeasty, cheese-pull, date-night Mexican dish featuring unsweetened coconut milk, nigiri-shaping, and diabetic-friendly ingredients. Perfect for a special occasion or romantic dinner.
instructions: Heat the paella pan over medium-high heat.
Add the ground beef and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the diced onion, bell pepper, and jalapeño pepper to the pan and cook until the vegetables are tender.
Add the cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper to the pan and stir to combine.
Add the paella rice to the pan and stir to combine with the beef and vegetables.
Add the chicken broth to the pan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes.
Stir in the unsweetened coconut milk and shredded Monterey Jack cheese.
Cook for an additional 5 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and the paella is heated through.
Transfer the paella to a serving platter and shape into a nigiri-style mound.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional shredded cheese and diced jalapeño pepper if desired.
